mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce household income and corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE increases month-over-month, or Rank IC is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy sharply elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 2.33% in August 2022 and rising
failure_condition: Average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is non-negative after FEDFUNDS first crosses above 4%, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining retail sales and rising unemployment identifies a consumer recession mechanism that amplifies downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS changes and UNRATE changes yields a 20-day Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals that restrictive policy is successfully suppressing real activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is above 3%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in headline CPI above 0.5% forces a rapid repricing of real yields and forward Fed expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day S&P 500 forward return is non-negative following months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeding 0.5%, or the directional accuracy is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects accelerating residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST increases month-over-month, or the Rank IC is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth combined with an accommodative federal funds rate below 3% signals robust earnings expansion without restrictive discount-rate headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with full employment
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS below 3% fails to outperform the unconditional baseline, with average 60-day returns below zero or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend signals aggregate demand outperformance that supports corporate revenue and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the Rank IC is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.
